prompts: To ensure home electricity safety, regularly check the state of your circuit breakers, as they protect electrical circuits from overloads and short circuits. Familiarize yourself with the location and function of each breaker. Install ground fault circuit interrupters (GFCIs) in bathrooms, kitchens, and outdoor areas to prevent electrical shocks by shutting off power in ground fault occurrences. Examine electrical appliances and cords for damage, and replace or repair them immediately to prevent hazards. Avoid overloading outlets with multiple high-wattage devices. Finally, educate household members about the importance of proper electrical usage and emergency procedures.
